evidence: Paper explicitly uses permutation-based importance, not impurity importance: "We report variable importance using a permutation-based approach...importance scores are computed as the reduction in the F1 score when each predictor is permuted." Since impurity importance bias is specific to Gini/MDI/variance methods, it does not apply. The paper does interpret magnitude and ranking centrally (e.g., "Prior viral load characteristics were identified as the most important variables"; "The model identified several viral testing variables that ranked as predictive over and above socio-demographic variables"), but this interpretation uses permutation importance which has different properties. Mediation analysis provides conditional validation of identified relationships.
